question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that triggers an HTTP redirect to an internal network address (SSRF), causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to retrieve node topology from an internal service and build a network map pointing to internal infrastructure, routing smart contract transactions to unintended internal endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to 'http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/' (AWS metadata endpoint) to exploit the lack of SSRF protection in the http.Client used by buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes(), causing the pinger to fetch cloud metadata and potentially expose credentials used to further compromise the network layer?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'OTHER' (uppercase) to bypass the strings.TrimSpace() normalization in loadConfig() since there is no strings.ToLower() call, causing the switch in newClient() to fall to the default error case and prevent the pinger from connecting to any network, resulting in a denial of smart contract monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to '0.0.0' (the treasury or system account) to cause the pinger to submit periodic transfers to a system-level account on the network layer, potentially triggering unintended smart contract interactions associated with system account operations?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to '0.0.0' (an invalid or system account), causing AccountIDFromString() in sdk_client.go to succeed but SetOperator() to configure an invalid operator, resulting in all subsequent network-layer transactions being submitted with a malformed payer that causes unintended smart contract fee-handling behavior?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a very large duration (e.g., '87600h') to effectively disable the pinger's network health monitoring, causing smart contract state changes on the network to go undetected and creating a blind spot in the layer 0/1/2 monitoring infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that envOr() returns the default value when the env var is all-whitespace, to silently override a legitimately set environment variable with the default operator key ('302e020100...'), causing the pinger to use a publicly known key for signing network-layer transactions that interact with smart contracts?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL with a path traversal component (e.g., 'http://mirror-rest:5551/../../evil') that, after strings.TrimRight() processing in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes(), constructs an unexpected API path that hits an unintended mirror node endpoint returning malicious network topology data?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to 0 and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_BASE_BACKOFF to '0s', causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to make exactly one attempt with no backoff, and if that attempt returns an empty node list, the pinger fails to build any network map, resulting in smart contract transactions never being submitted to the correct network layer?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope JSON with service_endpoints containing internal RFC-1918 IP addresses (e.g., '10.0.0.1'), causing the pinger to build a network map pointing to internal infrastructure and routing smart contract transactions to unintended internal gRPC services?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with a node_account_id of '0.0.2' (a known system account) mapped to an attacker-controlled gRPC endpoint, causing the SDK to route smart contract transactions intended for the system account to the attacker's server?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with service_endpoints containing port 0, which is filtered out by the ep.Port == 0 check in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork(), causing all nodes to be skipped and the 'no usable service_endpoints' error to trigger, resulting in the pinger failing to connect to any network layer node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with empty domain_name and empty ip_address_v4 for all service_endpoints, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to skip all endpoints and return 'no usable service_endpoints', preventing the pinger from establishing any network layer connection for smart contract transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with a very large number of nodes (e.g., 100,000 entries), causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to build an enormous network map that exhausts memory in the pinger process, resulting in a crash that disrupts smart contract monitoring on the network layer?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with service_endpoints containing a domain_name with special characters (e.g., null bytes or newlines) that survive strings.TrimSpace() and are passed to net.JoinHostPort(), causing malformed gRPC addresses in the network map that result in unintended connection behavior when submitting smart contract transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with service_endpoints containing an IPv6 address in the ip_address_v4 field, causing net.JoinHostPort() to produce a malformed address (missing brackets for IPv6) that the SDK fails to connect to, silently dropping those nodes from the network map and causing unintended smart contract transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with a node_account_id that parses successfully via AccountIDFromString() but maps to a non-existent account on the target network, causing the SDK to route smart contract transactions to nodes associated with phantom account IDs that produce unintended network-layer behavior?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with duplicate node_account_id entries mapping to different gRPC endpoints, causing the network map in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to silently overwrite earlier entries with later ones, resulting in non-deterministic smart contract transaction routing to potentially attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with a very large port number (e.g., 65536 or higher) in service_endpoints, causing net.JoinHostPort() to produce an address with an invalid port that the SDK silently accepts but fails to connect to, resulting in smart contract transactions being dropped at the network layer?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with a negative port number in service_endpoints, which passes the ep.Port == 0 check in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() (since it is non-zero), causing net.JoinHostPort() to produce a malformed address that results in unintended network layer connection failures for smart contract transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with a grpc_proxy_endpoint that is never used by fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() (only service_endpoints are consumed), causing the pinger to silently ignore the intended proxy routing and connect directly to nodes, bypassing network-layer proxy controls that are expected to mediate smart contract transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with a 'links.next' pagination field pointing to an attacker-controlled URL, and since fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() does not follow pagination, cause the pinger to build an incomplete network map that routes smart contract transactions only to a subset of nodes chosen by the attacker?
